name: TinyLaunch
description: A lightweight launch platform for indie makers that features one product a day with minimal friction. The audience is indie hackers and early adopters looking for fresh, small launches.

auth.notes: Sign up with email/password (Google sign-in often offered). Listings are scheduled for a launch day; a paid skip-the-line option may be available.

best_practices: Keep it lightweight and honest — TinyLaunch favors quick, no-fuss launches over polished corporate pitches.
Nail a single-sentence tagline; the daily feature gives you one line to earn the click.
Pick the right launch day and be ready to share the link with your own audience that day to drive votes.
Use a crisp square logo/icon that stands out in a minimal, text-light layout.
Keep the description short and concrete — what it does and who it helps, no filler.
Link to a working landing page or live app; reviewers skip dead or placeholder links.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: One short, clear sentence about the product.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: Briefly: what it does and who it's for.
